title: SAP Overview
learning_objectives: Understand the concept of Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP).
Learn what SAP is and why organizations use it.
Understand how SAP integrates different business departments.
Identify the major SAP modules used in manufacturing industries.
Understand SAP's role in digital transformation.

introduction.title: What is SAP?
introduction.content: SAP (Systems, Applications and Products in Data Processing) is one of the world's leading Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) software solutions. It enables organizations to manage their business operations by integrating departments such as Production, Finance, Materials Management, Sales, Human Resources, Quality, and Maintenance into a single system.

what_is_erp.title: What is ERP?
what_is_erp.content: Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) is a software system that connects different departments of an organization into a unified platform. Instead of maintaining separate systems for production, inventory, finance, purchasing, and sales, ERP allows all departments to work with a common database, ensuring accurate and real-time information across the organization.

why_sap.title: Why SAP?
points: Integrates all business functions into one system.
Reduces manual work and duplicate data entry.

sap_at_jsl.title: SAP at Jindal Stainless
sap_at_jsl.content: Jindal Stainless uses SAP as its enterprise platform to integrate manufacturing, production planning, quality management, inventory, procurement, finance, sales and other business processes. SAP enables seamless information flow between departments, ensuring efficient operations, improved productivity and data-driven decision making.

important_note: Every transaction performed in SAP updates a centralized database. This ensures that all authorized departments work with the same real-time information.
